title: Big bummer for me today
wikitext:
[]: Big bummer for me today             Quote Post by chaosakita » Thu Oct 18, 2012 5:55 pm Today, while I was looking for Evangelion sheet music files, I almost stumbled onto the file for Heisoku no Kakudai. It's the song that plays when Shinji exits Instrumentality during EoE and is considered to be one of the best songs of the series. After much work, I managed to get onto the archived version of the site that had the file. Much to my dismay, the Wayback Machine archived EVERY single file but that one. Yes, even the To Heart files had been archived over this. So much for nothing. I know a lot of people really enjoy that song, so I hope you can understand why I'm so bummed right now.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Thu Oct 18, 2012 6:20 pm This was one of my favorites from the soundtrack, too. It's a real shame that they didn't prioritize it.

[]:             Quote Post by Clover » Thu Oct 18, 2012 6:44 pm http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=5BdZhkc3ADY You can aaaaaaaaaalmost see it.

[]:             Quote Post by chaosakita » Thu Oct 18, 2012 7:49 pm Clover wrote: http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=5BdZhkc3ADY You can aaaaaaaaaalmost see it.

[]:             Quote Post by Clover » Thu Oct 18, 2012 8:21 pm chaosakita wrote: That's pretty good. But the version I was looking for had the parts for the entire orchestra. :(

[]:             Quote Post by chaosakita » Thu Oct 18, 2012 8:47 pm Clover wrote: Damn. That sounds like in-house material from the actual production. (Unless it's just a fan approximation of what should play what) That was uploaded to the internet? What do the surviving pages look like?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Oct 18, 2012 10:01 pm This probably isn't helpful, but I have a directory of sheet music. Maybe buried in there is something useful: http://www.evamonkey.com/sheet_music/ I don't think I've ever come across a book of Evangelion sheet music. If you have, let me know, I'd be interested in tracking it down and adding it to my collection for reference.

[]:             Quote Post by chaosakita » Thu Oct 18, 2012 10:07 pm No, the sheet music was the person's own transcription. My passion is making arrangements for string ensembles, because I was in one when I played the cello. I made an arrangement for Thanatos and I can't imagine why I didn't post it here.

[]:             Quote Post by TomasJC » Fri Oct 19, 2012 2:18 am Of all the EVA related scores I've found (mostly from Monkey's directory), I've never been able to locate this great piece. It's a shame that the file wasn't archived.

[]:             Quote Post by Clover » Fri Oct 19, 2012 6:14 am The Eva Monkey wrote: I don't think I've ever come across a book of Evangelion sheet music. If you have, let me know, I'd be interested in tracking it down and adding it to my collection for reference.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Fri Oct 19, 2012 7:28 am Clover wrote: http://animeartbookonline.com/evangelion-piano-all-eva-sheet-music-collection-book.html http://animeartbookonline.com/evangelion-song-collection-piano-sheet-music-book.html

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Oct 19, 2012 8:57 am riffraff11235 wrote: Those might be useful, but I think chaosakita said he was looking for the entire orchestra part.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Fri Oct 19, 2012 1:44 pm That truly is a shame. Heisoku no Kakudai is my favorite song of the series and I hope to one day do a recorded transcription of my own; working with the original composition to the orchestra piece could be of great use for that for me. That seems quite odd that this one in particular is the one missing, and tragic to boot. I wonder what the reason.

[]:             Quote Post by chaosakita » Fri Oct 19, 2012 4:30 pm The Wayback Machine didn't capture all the files on the page. :( I'm not giving up hope yet. I'm downloading midis intended for SRC (Simulated RPG) games, and I hope to find some Eva songs there. I've found a list of existing songs that I haven't found yet, so I'm going to keep looking for those. So far, I've found nothing but what seems to be Final Fantasy and Gundam songs. Also, I managed to find the files for Yume no Sukima, so I can transcribe those into sheet music.
